title: Australia demands AI companies must produce more energy than they consume, stop ‘theft’ of content
excerpt: PM frames sweeping new regulations as the equivalent of labour movement touchstones like winning a minimum wage
content_html:
AI AND ML

PM frames sweeping new regulations as the equivalent of labour movement touchstones like winning a minimum wage

Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has delivered a landmark speech outlining the nation’s AI policy, which will require datacenter builders to contribute more energy than they consume and mean AI companies must reach agreements with local artists and media before using their content.

“Let me make this crystal clear – not everything produced in Australia is up for grabs,” Albanese said, a reference to both content and the nation’s energy and water resources.

The PM said Australia will therefore legislate to require builders of large new datacenters to become net generators of energy, rather than consumers, by funding electricity generation projects to meet their needs and pay for associated work to bolster energy grids.

REG AD

The policy also requires datacenter operators to pay for water infrastructure and make minimal environmental impacts.

REG AD

The PM expects Australia’s states and territories to sign up to his plan so the nation can offer expedited approval processes for datacenter builds and consistent operating standards that apply across the country.

Nationwide laws, Albanese argued, will make Australia a more attractive destination for inbound investment by making it easier for AI companies to plan new datacenters – and perhaps offset other elements of the policy that are more onerous than laws in other countries.

“Australian writers, musicians, artists and journalists, must retain ownership and control of their work,” Albanese said. “Anything less is theft.”

He said Australia’s approach “will ensure Australian writers, artists and journalists retain ownership over their work, meaning no company should use Australian creative works to train AI without the artist’s control.”

The PM added his view that no country has given artists and rights-holders sufficient control of how AI companies use their works. Albanese didn’t say how he plans to enforce that control, but his speech framed the effort to do so as getting ahead of AI before big players get too much power.

Albanese asked his audience to imagine how much better off Australia would be if it had regulated social media a decade before the 2024 introduction of a ban on children aged under 16 accessing such services. He also compared the AI plan to past landmark reforms won by the global labor movement, such as winning a minimum wage and fixed working week.

The PM also said that without regulations of this sort, Australia will effectively outsource its security to big tech companies.

“If we are always dependent on someone else, somewhere else, we will be vulnerable,” he said. The AI policy aims to instead make Australia stronger.

REG AD

Albanese argued that Australians should not see AI as a threat to jobs, but that strong policy can make the technology a means to create new ones – beyond employment created by a short-term datacenter construction boom.

The PM wrapped his speech by suggesting AI can stand for “Australia’s Interest” as well as “artificial intelligence.” ®

sentences.zh: 总理希望澳大利亚各州和领地签署他的计划，以便该国能够为数据中心建设提供快速的审批流程以及适用于全国的一致运营标准。

sentences.zh: 阿尔巴尼斯认为，全国性法律将使人工智能公司更容易规划新的数据中心，从而使澳大利亚成为对入境投资更具吸引力的目的地，并且可能抵消政策中比其他国家法律更繁重的其他内容。

sentences.zh: 他表示，澳大利亚的做法“将确保澳大利亚作家、艺术家和记者保留对其作品的所有权，这意味着任何公司都不应在没有艺术家控制的情况下使用澳大利亚的创意作品来训练人工智能。”

Albanese didn’t say how he plans to enforce that control, but his speech framed the effort to do so as getting ahead of AI before big players get too much power.

sentences.en: Albanese didn’t say how he plans to enforce that control, but his speech framed the effort to do so as getting ahead of AI before big players get too much power.
sentences.zh: 阿尔巴尼斯没有透露他计划如何实施这种控制，但他的演讲将这样做的努力描述为在大公司获得太多权力之前领先于人工智能。

He also compared the AI plan to past landmark reforms won by the global labor movement, such as winning a minimum wage and fixed working week.

sentences.en: Albanese asked his audience to imagine how much better off Australia would be if it had regulated social media a decade before the 2024 introduction of a ban on children aged under 16 accessing such services.
sentences.zh: Albanese 让听众想象一下，如果澳大利亚在 2024 年禁止 16 岁以下儿童使用社交媒体的禁令之前十年就对社交媒体进行监管，情况会好得多。

sentences.en: He also compared the AI plan to past landmark reforms won by the global labor movement, such as winning a minimum wage and fixed working week.
sentences.zh: 他还将人工智能计划与全球劳工运动过去赢得的里程碑式改革进行了比较，例如赢得最低工资和固定工作周。

blocks.html_en: The PM also said that without regulations of this sort, Australia will effectively outsource its security to big tech companies.

sentences.en: The PM also said that without regulations of this sort, Australia will effectively outsource its security to big tech companies.
sentences.zh: 总理还表示，如果没有此类法规，澳大利亚实际上会将其安全外包给大型科技公司。

blocks.html_en: “If we are always dependent on someone else, somewhere else, we will be vulnerable,” he said. The AI policy aims to instead make Australia stronger.

sentences.en: “If we are always dependent on someone else, somewhere else, we will be vulnerable,” he said.
sentences.zh: “如果我们总是在其他地方依赖别人，我们就会很脆弱，”他说。

sentences.en: The AI policy aims to instead make Australia stronger.
sentences.zh: 相反，人工智能政策的目的是让澳大利亚变得更强大。

blocks.html_en: Albanese argued that Australians should not see AI as a threat to jobs, but that strong policy can make the technology a means to create new ones – beyond employment created by a short-term datacenter construction boom.

sentences.en: Albanese argued that Australians should not see AI as a threat to jobs, but that strong policy can make the technology a means to create new ones – beyond employment created by a short-term datacenter construction boom.
sentences.zh: Albanese 认为，澳大利亚人不应将人工智能视为对就业的威胁，但强有力的政策可以使该技术成为创造新就业机会的手段，而不仅仅是短期数据中心建设热潮所创造的就业机会。

blocks.html_en: The PM wrapped his speech by suggesting AI can stand for “Australia’s Interest” as well as “artificial intelligence.” ®

sentences.en: The PM wrapped his speech by suggesting AI can stand for “Australia’s Interest” as well as “artificial intelligence.” ®
sentences.zh: 总理在演讲结束时表示，人工智能可以代表“澳大利亚的利益”和“人工智能”。 ®

content_html_zh:
人工智能和机器学习

总理将全面的新法规视为劳工运动的试金石，例如赢得最低工资

澳大利亚总理安东尼·阿尔巴内塞发表了具有里程碑意义的演讲，概述了国家的人工智能政策，该政策将要求数据中心建设者贡献比消耗更多的能源，并意味着人工智能公司在使用其内容之前必须与当地艺术家和媒体达成协议。

阿尔巴内斯说：“让我说得非常清楚——并非澳大利亚生产的所有产品都可以争夺。”他指的是内容以及国家的能源和水资源。

总理表示，澳大利亚将因此立法要求大型新数据中心的建设者成为能源的净生产者，而不是消费者，通过资助发电项目来满足他们的需求，并支付相关工作的费用来支持能源网络。

REG AD

该政策还要求数据中心运营商支付水利基础设施费用，并尽量减少对环境的影响。

REG AD

总理希望澳大利亚各州和领地签署他的计划，以便该国能够为数据中心建设提供快速的审批流程以及适用于全国的一致运营标准。

阿尔巴尼斯认为，全国性法律将使人工智能公司更容易规划新的数据中心，从而使澳大利亚成为对入境投资更具吸引力的目的地，并且可能抵消政策中比其他国家法律更繁重的其他内容。

“澳大利亚作家、音乐家、艺术家和记者必须保留对其作品的所有权和控制权，”阿尔巴尼斯说。 “少了就是盗窃。”

他表示，澳大利亚的做法“将确保澳大利亚作家、艺术家和记者保留对其作品的所有权，这意味着任何公司都不应在没有艺术家控制的情况下使用澳大利亚的创意作品来训练人工智能。”

总理补充说，他认为没有一个国家给予艺术家和权利持有者足够的控制人工智能公司如何使用其作品的控制权。 阿尔巴尼斯没有透露他计划如何实施这种控制，但他的演讲将这样做的努力描述为在大公司获得太多权力之前领先于人工智能。

Albanese 让听众想象一下，如果澳大利亚在 2024 年禁止 16 岁以下儿童使用社交媒体的禁令之前十年就对社交媒体进行监管，情况会好得多。 他还将人工智能计划与全球劳工运动过去赢得的里程碑式改革进行了比较，例如赢得最低工资和固定工作周。

总理还表示，如果没有此类法规，澳大利亚实际上会将其安全外包给大型科技公司。

“如果我们总是在其他地方依赖别人，我们就会很脆弱，”他说。 相反，人工智能政策的目的是让澳大利亚变得更强大。

REG AD

Albanese 认为，澳大利亚人不应将人工智能视为对就业的威胁，但强有力的政策可以使该技术成为创造新就业机会的手段，而不仅仅是短期数据中心建设热潮所创造的就业机会。

总理在演讲结束时表示，人工智能可以代表“澳大利亚的利益”和“人工智能”。 ®
